title: [PtrUseVisitor] Support llvm.structured.gep
author: Keenuts
description: This commit adds support the @llvm.structued.gep instruction when computing a pointer offset. Even if the physical layout of the pointed element is not yet known (hence SGEP), we can still determine which element is being accessed. So the offset value here is coherent in the context of LLVM, which is the important part.

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

%struct.S = type { i32, float }

declare ptr @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr, ...)

define void @test_escape(ptr %escape) {
entry:
  %alloca = alloca %struct.S, align 4
  %gep = call ptr (ptr, ...) @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr elementtype(%struct.S) %alloca, i32 1)
  store ptr %gep, ptr %escape, align 8
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

declare ptr @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr, ...)

define i32 @test_dynamic_index(i32 %idx) {
entry:
  %a = alloca [10 x i32], align 4
  %ptr = call ptr (ptr, ...) @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr elementtype([10 x i32]) %a, i32 %idx)
  store i32 42, ptr %ptr, align 4
  %val = load i32, ptr %ptr, align 4
  ret i32 %val
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

%struct.S = type { i32, float }

declare ptr @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr, ...)

define float @test_struct_extern(ptr %memory) {
entry:
  %gep1 = call ptr (ptr, ...) @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr elementtype(%struct.S) %memory, i32 1)
  store float 2.000000e+00, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %val = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  ret float %val
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

declare ptr @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr, ...)

define i16 @test_array() {
entry:
  %a = alloca [4 x i8], align 1
  store float 0.000000e+00, ptr %a, align 4
  %ptr = call ptr (ptr, ...) @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr elementtype([4 x i8]) %a, i32 2)
  %val = load i16, ptr %ptr, align 2
  ret i16 %val
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

%struct.S = type { i32, float }

declare ptr @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr, ...)

define float @test_struct() {
entry:
  %alloca = alloca %struct.S, align 4
  %gep0 = call ptr (ptr, ...) @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr elementtype(%struct.S) %alloca, i32 0)
  store i32 1, ptr %gep0, align 4
  %gep1 = call ptr (ptr, ...) @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr elementtype(%struct.S) %alloca, i32 1)
  store float 2.000000e+00, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %val = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  ret float %val
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

declare ptr @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr, ...)

define i32 @test_nested_struct() {
entry:
  %X = alloca { i32, { float, i32 } }, align 4
  %Y = call ptr (ptr, ...) @llvm.structured.gep.p0(ptr elementtype({ i32, { float, i32 } }) %X, i32 1, i32 1)
  store i32 0, ptr %Y, align 4
  %Z = load i32, ptr %Y, align 4
  ret i32 %Z
}

comments.author: Keenuts
